ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.

ElementDescription: Sex of subject at birth
Notes: M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: Please indicate whether you think each of the following statements is true or false: It is possible to see a gene with the naked eye.
Please indicate whether you think each of the following statements is true or false: Healthy parents can have a child with a hereditary disease.
Please indicate whether you think each of the following statements is true or false: The onset of certain diseases is due to genes, environment and lifestyle.
Please indicate whether you think each of the following statements is true or false: The carrier of a disease gene may be completely healthy.
Please indicate whether you think each of the following statements is true or false: All serious diseases are hereditary.
Please indicate whether you think each of the following statements is true or false: Genes are inside cells.
Please indicate whether you think each of the following statements is true or false: The child of a disease gene carrier is always also a carrier of the same disease.
Please indicate whether you think each of the following statements is true or false: A gene is a piece of DNA.
Please indicate whether you think each of the following statements is true or false: A gene is part of a chromosome.
Please indicate whether you think each of the following statements is true or false: All body parts have all of the same genes.
Please indicate whether you think each of the following statements is true or false: It has been estimated that a person has about 20,000 genes.
Please indicate whether you think each of the following statements is true or false: A person's race and ethnicity can affect how likely they are to get a disease.
Please indicate whether you think each of the following statements is true or false: Each of us has variations in our genes that make it more likely that we will get certain diseases.
Please indicate whether you think each of the following statements is true or false: A ''complex disease'' is a health condition brought on by many genes and lifestyle and environment.

Please indicate whether you think each of the following statements is true or false: A single nucleotide polymorphism or ''SNiP'' is a variation present in some individuals that stretches across a large section of DNA.
